pages.raw_text:
ployed in the case. They have been
employed by fiocks and shoals, by
both the prosecution and defense.
Numbers of them have been en-
camped here ever since the begin
ming to keep tab on the main fig
ures in the case and to learn the
business Of strangers coming here
as prospective witnesses for con-
sultation with the lawyers. No
town even in Russia was ever more
completely under the eye of secret
service agents. .ts


There has been no lack of funds
for the preparation of the case
The legislature appropriated $780,
900 for the prosecution and the bill
that is accommodating against the
state will crowd the capitol when
it is carried in


The prisoners have been pro-
vided with funds by the working
men of the country, There was an
uprising of the workers when the
prisoners were kidnapped from Colo
RADIO and it has not subsidized. The
workingmen have reinforced their
resolutions by contributing money
sufli cient to enable the prisoners to
meet the tremendous resources of
the opposition.


Both sides have secured all the
western country for witnesses.
Secret agents and lawyers have
been engaged in the hunt. They
have visited every mining camp In
Idaho. Utah, Colorado, Wyoming
and Montana that offered the
slightest hope of evidence. They
not only prosecuted original re-
search. but, SO far as possible,
trailed the agents of the opposition.
